name: agentlens-dashboard
description: Observability for AI agent sessions — OTEL traces and local log file ingestion. VS Code extension, npx, and Docker.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with AgentLens
walkthroughs.description: Observe and debug your AI agent sessions in real time.

steps.title: What is AgentLens?
steps.description:
AgentLens gives you a live observability dashboard for GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, and Codex — token usage, latency, file changes, and loop detection.

[Open AgentLens Sidebar](command:workbench.view.extension.agent-lens)

steps.title: Agents Are Ready
steps.description: AgentLens automatically configured your agents to send telemetry to the local collector. Restart any running sessions to start collecting traces. Past session history is already loading from local log files — no restart needed to see it.

steps.title: Start an Agent Session
steps.description: Run Copilot, Claude Code, or Codex and the dashboard will populate in real time.

steps.title: Open the Full Dashboard
steps.description:
The sidebar shows a live summary. Open the full 16-tab dashboard in an editor panel for traces, diffs, alerts, cost estimation, export, and more.

[Open Dashboard](command:agentLens.openDashboard)

steps.title: Detect Loops and Inefficiencies
steps.description: The Sessions tab surfaces five failure patterns per session — deadlocks, error spirals, context accumulation, and more. The Advisor tab analyzes patterns across sessions to generate specific suggestions for improving your agent instruction file.

agentLens.otlpPort.description: Local port for OTLP trace receiver

agentLens.autoConfigureAgents.description: Automatically write OTEL telemetry settings into Claude Code's (~/.claude/settings.json), Codex's (~/.codex/config.toml), and GitHub Copilot's own configuration so they send session data to AgentLens. Disabling this leaves your agents' configuration untouched — you can still configure OTEL manually (see the Help tab) or rely on agentLens.enableLogIngestion alone. Codex and Copilot CLI sessions have little to show without OTEL: their log files carry only aggregate totals, no per-turn tool calls or responses.

agentLens.enableOtelIngestion.description: Accept incoming OTEL span data. The OTLP server keeps listening on agentLens.otlpPort regardless; disabling this silently drops received payloads without storing them.

agentLens.enableLogIngestion.description: Read local session logs for Claude Code, Codex, and Copilot CLI as a data source. Log data has lower fidelity than OTEL — timing, speed, and loop signals are not available for Codex and Copilot CLI, whose log files carry only aggregate totals. Log and OTEL sessions are shown as separate entries rather than merged. Loading is done incrementally in the background and will not block the UI.

agentLens.enableMcpServer.description: Start the AgentLens MCP server so Claude Code can query your session history. The server listens on agentLens.mcpPort (default 4316). See the AgentLens Help tab → Agent Integration for setup instructions.

agentLens.mcpPort.description: Local port for the AgentLens MCP server (when agentLens.enableMcpServer is true). Configure Claude Code to connect with: { "mcpServers": { "agentlens": { "url": "http://localhost:4316/mcp" } } }

scripts.compile: pnpm run check-types && pnpm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.fixtures:check: node demo/generate-fixtures.js --dir /tmp/agentlens-fixtures && pnpm run compile-tests && node demo/validate-fixtures.js --dir /tmp/agentlens-fixtures
scripts.package: pnpm run check-types && pnpm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint
